steps: Log in to your Moodle site as an administrator
Go to Site Administration > Server > Web services > Overview and follow the setup wizard to enable web services
Go to Site Administration > Server > Web services > Manage protocols and enable the REST protocol
Go to Site Administration > Server > Web services > External services and create or select a service with the required functions
Go to Site Administration > Server > Web services > Manage tokens
Click 'Add' to create a new token — select the user and the external service
Copy the generated token and use it as the 'wstoken' query parameter in API requests

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Web Service Token
credential_fields.description: Your Moodle web service token (wstoken), generated in Site Administration

notes: Moodle is self-hosted — there is no central cloud API; the base URL is your own Moodle instance domain
Tokens cannot be created for administrator accounts for security reasons
Web services must be explicitly enabled in Site Administration before tokens work
The token is passed as the 'wstoken' query parameter on every request
